obligations.reason: The fact layer shows key_in/key_big and inter_state feeding external_bus_io.rdata at multiple address cases, so visible RTL should be checked for whether these protected values are reachable by the APB/register subject without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/register requester via external_bus_io
obligations.object: AES key material key_in/key_big and intermediate state inter_state
obligations.expected_guard: Key and AES intermediate-state readback should be blocked or mediated by an explicit authorization/debug/lifecycle guard; normal software-readable status/data registers should not expose key_in/key_big or inter_state unless authorized.
obligations.candidate_path: external_bus_io APB/reg interface -> aes_wrapper read mux -> external_bus_io.rdata
state_conditions_to_check: address values selecting key_big[191:0] or inter_state[127:0]
any debug/test/lifecycle authorization state that gates readback
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer is truncated around top-level address routing, so confirm exact APB decode and requester privilege context in ariane_peripherals.

obligations.reason: Assignments show external_bus_io.wdata updates AES control and data/state registers, requiring inspection of the write-enable/address guard and whether protected AES state can be overwritten by unprivileged or unintended subjects.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/register requester via external_bus_io
obligations.object: AES control start, plaintext p_c, and state registers
obligations.expected_guard: Configuration of AES plaintext/control/state registers should be restricted to intended bus writes and, if state is protected, mediated by privilege, lock, or lifecycle controls; writes should not occur from reads or unqualified bus activity.
obligations.candidate_path: external_bus_io APB/reg interface -> aes_wrapper write logic -> p_c/state/start -> aes_192_sed inputs
state_conditions_to_check: ct_valid/busy state if configuration changes during operation
required_evidence_slots: address decode for each writable register
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection to determine whether pwrite/psel/penable or equivalent guards are used around the shown assignments.

obligations.reason: The scope includes SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, SimDTM, debug request signals, and AXI master/slave connects; check whether debug-originated transactions are authorized before accessing protected objects.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester
obligations.object: SoC memory/peripheral address space, including bootrom, rom2, AES, and security-sensitive MMIO
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI debug accesses that can issue memory/peripheral operations should be gated by debug authentication/authorization or lifecycle/test-mode policy before reaching protected address ranges.
obligations.candidate_path: debug/JTAG/DTM/DMI request path -> debug module AXI master/connect -> SoC memory/peripheral fabric
state_conditions_to_check: init_done or lifecycle-like state if present
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists instances and signals but not full debug module internals; inspect wiring in testharness to establish reachability.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows jtag_key as a 32-bit signal in the testharness and as an output port of ariane_peripherals, suggesting a permission token whose exposure and use as a guard must be checked.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug interface and any external/software observer of jtag_key
obligations.expected_guard: Any JTAG unlock key or debug authorization token should not be exposed to unauthorized software-visible or external outputs, and use of the key should be tied to an authentication comparison rather than a mere signal presence.
obligations.candidate_path: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> jtag_key signal -> ariane_peripherals jtag_key output or internal guard
state_conditions_to_check: when key is loaded or changed
required_evidence_slots: whether jtag_key is externally observable or bus-readable
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect whether jtag_key is a secret, public status, or derived key and whether it actually mediates any protected operation.

obligations.reason: The wrapper exposes priv_lvl_i and connectivity_mapping plus direct assignments of AXI address/prot/valid channels; inspect whether privilege and map outputs actually gate AW/AR routing and not just annotate signals.
obligations.subject: AXI managers including core/debug masters
obligations.object: AXI subordinate address regions and protected peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: Access from managers to subordinate address regions should be mediated by privilege level, connectivity map, address-range checks, and security attributes; privilege must be consistently applied to read and write channels.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI manager/slave interfaces -> connectivity_mapping / axi_node_intf_wrap -> subordinate ports
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer includes many direct channel assignments; full routing and connectivity logic must be inspected for actual gating.

obligations.reason: rom2 has req_i,we_i,addr_i,wdata_i,rdata_o and ariane_peripherals has pkey_loc_o near rom2 wiring; check whether write inputs are ignored/protected and whether key-location data is exposed only as intended.
obligations.subject: APB/AXI requester reaching rom2
obligations.object: rom2 memory contents and pkey_loc_o/key-location related data
obligations.expected_guard: ROM-like protected contents should ignore or reject writes and should only expose intended read data for authorized address ranges; any pkey/key-location output should be mediated if sensitive.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/APB decode -> rom2 instance -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata/rdata
required_evidence_slots: write enable use or ignore behavior
obligations.uncertainty: Need source inspection to determine whether rom2 is purely combinational/read-only and how pkey_loc_o is assigned.

obligations.reason: bootrom exposes req_i,we_i,wdata_i and rdata_o, while facts show raddr_q and rdata_o assignment; visible RTL should confirm writes are ignored and reads are range-checked.
obligations.subject: AXI/requester reaching bootrom
obligations.object: bootrom memory contents and boot code read data
obligations.expected_guard: Boot ROM should be immutable from bus writes and read access should be limited to intended address range; write-enable inputs should not alter ROM contents or bypass boot policy.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/testharness bus -> bootrom instance -> bootrom req/we/addr/wdata/rdata
obligations.uncertainty: Assignments suggest read-only behavior, but inspect full module for any mem write blocks or side effects.

obligations.reason: The scope includes multiple apb_to_reg instances and psel/pwrite/penable/paddr/prdata signals; verify that address selection and privilege/security attributes are not lost when converting bus transactions to register accesses.
obligations.subject: software-visible bus requester through APB bridge
obligations.object: APB-visible peripheral registers including AES and interrupt/control registers
obligations.expected_guard: APB peripheral selection and write/read enables should include correct address decode and, for protected peripherals such as AES and interrupt control, expected privilege or policy checks before register access.
obligations.candidate_path: APB bridge/decode in ariane_peripherals -> PLIC/packet/AES/UART register blocks
obligations.uncertainty: Need exact address map and bridge wiring from ariane_peripherals.

obligations.reason: test_en appears in the testharness and as test_en_i on axi_node_intf_wrap; check whether it bypasses permission checks or reset guards on paths reaching protected objects.
obligations.subject: test interface or environment-controlled test_en
obligations.object: protected access-control checks, debug paths, and reset-controlled security state
obligations.expected_guard: Test enable should not open debug access, bypass connectivity checks, or alter resets for protected state unless constrained to authorized test/lifecycle mode.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer gives signal presence but not all fanout; inspect source to distinguish benign scan/test reset use from access-control bypass.

obligations.reason: The scope includes ndmreset, rstgen, AES reset and readable crypto outputs; check whether debug resets or partial resets leave sensitive state readable via APB/JTAG paths.
obligations.subject: debug/JTAG reset requester and software bus requester
obligations.object: AES key/intermediate/ciphertext/status state and debug/security control state
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-triggered or non-debug resets should clear or preserve security-sensitive state according to policy and should not leave stale key/intermediate/output data externally readable after reset or mode transition.
obligations.candidate_path: debug reset ndmreset / rstgen outputs -> core/peripherals/AES/ROM/debug state
obligations.uncertainty: Need reset wiring and AES instance reset behavior; key_in may be external and not reset locally.
